verified_take: GAN plus transforms improve in-domain WBC classification on LISC.
relevance_to_our_project: Shows WBC synthetic augmentation is already established.
gap_vs_our_claim: No leakage-safe domain generalization or policy benchmark.

verified_take: Generative models are used inside a WBC classification pipeline.
relevance_to_our_project: Confirms WBC generation-plus-classification is not new.
gap_vs_our_claim: Still centered on in-domain classification instead of unseen-domain utility.

verified_take: Shows that WBC classifiers do not generalize cleanly across datasets.
relevance_to_our_project: Justifies our LODO and held-out domain framing.
gap_vs_our_claim: Does not propose synthetic augmentation or generation policy.

primary_task: Imbalanced domain generalization for hematological cytomorphology classification
verified_take: Classifier-centric WBC DG under domain shift and imbalance.
relevance_to_our_project: Shows WBC DG itself already has prior art.
gap_vs_our_claim: Not a synthetic generation policy paper.

primary_task: Test-time augmentation for cross-domain leukocyte classification
verified_take: Cross-domain gains come from OOD filtering plus self-ensembling at test time.
relevance_to_our_project: Strong nearby comparison point for cross-domain WBC improvement.
gap_vs_our_claim: No training-time synthetic pool design or subset utility evaluation.
source_note: Author metadata recovered from DOI BibTeX.

primary_task: Diffusion/LoRA cytomorphology synthesis plus low-data classification
verified_take: Diffusion-based WBC/cytomorphology generation can improve low-data classification.
relevance_to_our_project: Closest external precedent for our generation backbone family.
gap_vs_our_claim: Not evaluated as leakage-safe multi-domain DG utility.

primary_task: Physics-inspired GAN for rare WBC classification in FPM
verified_take: Physics-aware synthetic generation improves rare basophil classification in FPM.
relevance_to_our_project: Shows rare-class augmentation and physics-aware generation are also prior art.
gap_vs_our_claim: Different modality and not smear-image DG or synthetic policy selection.

primary_task: Stain-aware augmentation and domain alignment for blood cell classification
verified_take: Uses stain-aware augmentation plus domain alignment and contrastive learning for WBC DG.
relevance_to_our_project: Most direct external WBC DG competitor.
gap_vs_our_claim: Feature-learning centric; no synthetic image generation policy benchmark.
source_note: arXiv uses 'Imbalance Blood Cell Classification'; repo subtitle says 'Imbalanced Blood Cell Classification'.

primary_task: General medical imaging DG classification via regularization
verified_take: Representation regularization is a strong non-generative DG baseline in medical imaging.
relevance_to_our_project: Helps frame why generation policy needs its own justification.
gap_vs_our_claim: Not WBC-specific and not synthetic augmentation.

verified_take: Policy-level augmentation redesign can itself be the paper contribution.
relevance_to_our_project: Important precedent for claiming novelty at the augmentation-policy level.
gap_vs_our_claim: Segmentation setting, not WBC classification or synthetic image generation.
source_note: arXiv preprint is 2022; official repo frames it as AAAI 2023.

primary_task: Style-content decomposition augmentation for DG segmentation
verified_take: Explicitly separates style and content shifts for DG augmentation.
relevance_to_our_project: Closest external analogy to our cell-preservation versus background-shift thinking.
gap_vs_our_claim: Segmentation task; not WBC synthetic pool utility.
source_note: arXiv is 2025; repo citation points to MIDL 2026.

primary_task: Anatomy-preserving style transfer for DG classification and TTA
verified_take: Style diversity plus anatomy preservation is already an active DG augmentation direction.
relevance_to_our_project: Constrains our novelty: preservation-aware generation alone is not enough.
gap_vs_our_claim: Not WBC-specific and not a leakage-safe synthetic subset study.
source_note: arXiv preprint is 2026; repo labels it ISBI 2026.
